reference_suggested_rewrite: In the reported main evaluation, generated benchmarks achieved about 96-98% human acceptance and UIA scores from 68.54 to 81.48.
The generated benchmarks are reported to show consistent Qwen3.5 scaling trends on three applicable benchmarks from 2B to 27B.
On TeleSWEBench, the best-performing evaluated ASE tools reached up to a 25% success rate for producing shippable code.
TeleSWEBench uses a two-stage evaluation pipeline designed to decouple file-localization or architectural navigation skills from functional code-generation correctness.
On RewardBench, SenseJudge outperformed compared general LLM-as-a-judge methods and was 1.65% behind the generative reward model without training.
The paper proposes SenseBench as a diverse instruction-following benchmark derived from real-world multi-turn interactions.
The proposed method is reported to improve over Qwen3-VL-4B-Thinking by 4% in pairwise evaluation and 11% in batch-level evaluation.
The ablation reports that using PPJD instead of MMPR-v1.2 as training data improves all measured metrics.
Across evaluated models, the more consistent pattern was a sharp rise in deflection, where responses were neither harmful completions nor explicit refusals.
The paper introduces TukaBench as a jailbreak benchmark for seven African languages that extends JailbreakBench beyond direct translation through four settings.
In RUT-Bench experiments, no tested LLM exceeded 40% overall success, and nearly all dropped under more complicated non-ideal user inputs.
RUT-Bench uses deterministic Python class environments that abstract away the operational friction of real-world external APIs.
After validation and deduplication, the SemRepo construction pipeline obtained 197,566 unique repository URLs.
SemRepo links 11,867 contributors, approximately 6% of all persons in SemRepo, enabling joint analysis of software development activity and bibliometric author profiles.
Among the surveyed materials science journals, 98% had a research data policy and 95% mentioned a data availability statement.
In the surveyed journals, code sharing was encouraged but optional for 70% and required for only 1.8%.
Participants found ERSmeta's elements useful and relevant, but identified the high number of elements and system complexity as significant drawbacks.
ERSmeta consists of 86 top-level elements clustered into ten thematic areas.
The paper reports that resources required to reproduce and extend the work are openly available in a curated Mendeley Data repository.
The GitHub Repository Extraction pipeline crawled 37,176 repositories and selected 4,610 based on predefined inclusion criteria.
OpenDORS v2025-11 contains 134,352 research software projects based on repository URLs mined from 2,656,582 PMC, ArXiv, and JOSS publications.
The authors release the OpenDORS dataset and construction workflow under an open license, and also release the open-source Python package opendors2 implementing the construction logic.
The Software Observatory initially ingests metadata from registries such as bio.tools, Bioconda, Bioconductor, Galaxy ToolShed, bioinformatics-tagged SourceForge, and Galaxy Europe.
The Software Observatory initially collected over 65,942 metadata records from major Life Sciences registries, including bio.tools, Bioconda, and the Galaxy ToolShed.
ORCE uses a sampling-based surrogate of correctness likelihood to supervise verbalized confidence without relying on internal model probabilities.
ORCE's practical algorithm uses SC to construct preference pairs, and DPO performs a smoothed preference-optimization step rather than directly maximizing expected SC reward.
The method uses self-consistency offline on unlabeled data to construct supervision for a single-pass confidence predictor, reducing inference-time use to a single generation.
The evaluation covers 36 model/dataset combinations: 9 models on 2 math datasets and 6 models on 3 QA datasets.
The fused confidence method primarily functions as a post-hoc recalibration tool and yields substantial ECE reductions across most benchmarks.
The fusion method's AUROC and AUPRC-N improvements are inconsistent and highly model-sensitive.
Global-threshold conformal prediction for LLMs can over- or under-cover specific inputs despite valid marginal guarantees.
In long-form QA experiments, the adaptive conformal procedure preserves marginal coverage while improving conditional coverage and consistency across categories.
Code language models frequently produce overconfident incorrect predictions and underconfident correct predictions, undermining deployment reliability.
The paper introduces a framework that integrates uncertainty estimation, model calibration, and tool-based abstention handling for code models.
Uncertainty reliability degrades under domain shift, while GNLL-based probabilistic modeling, ensembling, and recalibration each help improve OOD uncertainty awareness.
Among the evaluated OOD datasets, Sensors has the largest SBP distribution shift relative to the training distribution, followed by UCI, PPGBP, and BCG.
